title: Migration script has +x but still gets 'Permission denied'
description_md:
Running database migrations via `run-migration` fails with `Permission denied` -- even though the script it calls is owned correctly and `ls -l` clearly shows the execute bit set. Nobody changed the script itself. SSH in as `trainee` (passwordless `sudo`), work out why the execute bit isn't enough here, and get migrations running again (you do not have the ability to remount filesystems in this environment -- find a fix that doesn't require it).

You're done when running `run-migration` actually completes and leaves its marker file.
